# moodle-cli 0.3.0

## Summary

This release is focused on forum workflows for agent use.

It adds a narrower and more reliable path from "I have a clue" to "I have the target discussion", improves grouped forum handling on real Moodle sites, and exposes more structured forum content so agents can consume discussions without brittle text parsing.

## Highlights

### New forum discovery flow

Forum support now includes a dedicated discovery stack:

- `moodle forum discussions`
- `moodle forum search`
- `moodle forum find`
- `moodle forum check`

The intended default for agent use is:

1. `moodle forum find QUERY --json`
2. add `--list --limit N` when one hit is not enough
3. add `--body` only when the snippet is insufficient

This reduces unnecessary forum enumeration and keeps the default path small.

### `moodle forum find`

`moodle forum find` is now the shortest-path entry point for forum lookups.

It supports:

- course narrowing with `--course`
- forum narrowing with `--forum`
- unread prioritisation with `--unread-only`
- shortlist mode with `--list`
- direct expansion with `--body`
- scan budgets with `--limit-forums` and `--limit-discussions`

This is designed for agent requests such as:

- "Find the post about attendance codes"
- "Show me the most recent unread forum update"
- "Give me a shortlist of likely matches"

### Grouped forum support

Some real Moodle forums use separate groups and show an empty default view even when discussions exist in another group.

`moodle-cli` now discovers visible forum groups and aggregates discussion refs across them automatically.

This fixes real cases where:

- `forum discussions FORUM_ID` previously returned `[]`
- the actual discussions were only visible after selecting a group in the Moodle UI

Discussion lists now also show the originating group in terminal output.

### More structured discussion extraction

Forum discussion output now exposes more structure for agent consumers.

Posts include:

- `image_urls`
- `links`
- `tables`

Discussion payloads also include group context when available:

- `group_id`
- `group_name`

This makes forum discussions usable as a general extraction tool for:

- table-based announcements
- posts with image-only content
- grouped tutorial/workshop discussions

## User-visible changes

- Added `moodle forum find`
- Added shortlist mode to `moodle forum find` with `--list`
- Added forum scan budgets with `--limit-forums` and `--limit-discussions`
- Added automatic grouped forum discovery
- Added `Group` to forum discussion list tables
- Added structured `links` and `tables` fields to forum discussion posts
- Added repository skill guidance for forum agent routing

## Examples

```bash
moodle forum find "attendance code" --course 40406 --json
moodle forum find "attendance code" --course 40406 --list --limit 3 --json
moodle forum find "attendance code" --course 40406 --body --json
moodle forum discussions 5235445 --json
moodle forum discussion "https://learning.monash.edu/mod/forum/discuss.php?d=608420" --json
```

## Validation

Validated with:

- `uv run pytest -q`
- `uv build`
- real-site checks against grouped forum pages on Monash ELMS
- real-site checks against discussion URLs containing table-based attendance posts

## Upgrade notes

No configuration changes are required.

Agents using the root `SKILL.md` should prefer `moodle forum find` as the default forum entry point and treat `forum discussion` as the structured expansion step.
